channel.title: NYT > Business
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: China’s Housing Slump Shows Signs of Bottoming Out. We’ve Been Here Before.
item.description: Property prices in Shanghai, in particular, are rebounding, but the national market still faces an enormous overhang — 90 million empty or unfinished apartments.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:description: Many families in China poured their savings into apartments once seen as a safe path to wealth, only to watch values unravel.

item.title: Musk’s SpaceX Reveals Its Finances for the First Time as It Readies for IPO
item.description: Mr. Musk’s rocket and satellite maker disclosed its financial performance as it prepares to go public in what is set to be one of the largest offerings to date.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Lauren Hirsch
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: A successful SpaceX offering would bring riches to Wall Street, the company’s employees and, of course, Elon Musk, who is already the world’s richest person.

item.title: TSA Pushes to Expand Private Security at Airport Checkpoints
item.description: Driven in part by the chaos of the last shutdown, the government is expanding efforts to use private security staff instead of federal employees at more airports.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung
item.media:description: After the last government shutdown caused long lines at Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport, the Atlanta City Council moved to study a private screening model.

item.title: Nvidia’s Profit Hits $58.3 Billion as A.I. Boom Gathers More Steam
item.description: The chip maker said its profit in its most recent quarter jumped 211 percent from a year earlier thanks to extreme demand from other big technology companies.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle
item.media:credit: Manuel Orbegozo for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jensen Huang, Nvidia’s chief executive, made a bet years ago on computer chips that would be useful for building artificial intelligence.

item.title: Majority of Fed Officials Embraced Possibility of Higher Rates at Latest Meeting
item.description: A record of the April meeting, Jerome H. Powell’s last as chair, underscored the extent to which the war with Iran has upended the economic outlook.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Federal Reserve meeting in April was Jerome H. Powell’s last as chair.

item.title: Jeff Bezos Praises Trump’s Second Term as ‘More Mature’
item.description: The Amazon founder and owner of The Washington Post also rejected the idea that he has made changes at his companies to gain favor with the president.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson

item.title: Barney Frank, Gay Pioneer and Liberal Stalwart in Congress, Dies at 86
item.description: Often voted the “brainiest,” “funniest” and “most eloquent” member of the House, he was also the first to come out voluntarily and helped normalize being openly gay in public office.
item.dc:creator: Katharine Q. Seelye
item.category: Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act (2010)

item.title: To 2026 Graduates Facing a Bleak Job Market, 1991 Grads Have Some Advice
item.description: The New York Times profiled young job seekers in 1991. Today, their own children are entering a work force in flux.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Ember
item.category: Rutgers, The State University of New Jersey
item.media:credit: via Sharon Dilling; Michelle Gustafson for The New York Times

item.title: James Murdoch Buys Half of Vox Media
item.description: The media scion is buying Vox Media’s podcast network, New York magazine and Vox.com for more than $300 million.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin and Jessica Testa
item.media:credit: Victor Llorente for The New York Times
item.media:description: James Murdoch left, and Jim Bankoff, right.

item.title: The Growing Anxiety Over AI, Jobs and the Future
item.description: Layoffs linked to artificial intelligence are rattling workers and college graduates, stoking concerns worldwide that are showing up in polls.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly and Brian O’Keefe
item.media:description: A.I.-related layoffs at Meta may be feeding into rising global anxiety about the consequences of companies embracing the technology.

item.title: Is the Bond Market Signaling Danger? Or Opportunity?
item.description: Interest rates for long-term Treasury bonds have surged to levels last seen in 2007, before the great financial crisis.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Sommer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: A Founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics Gave Up the High Life to Be a Catholic Priest
item.description: A mystical encounter 12 years ago led Scott Vincent Borba, a founder of E.L.F. Beauty, on a journey that will culminate this week, when he is ordained as a Roman Catholic priest.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson
item.media:description: Scott Vincent Borba was ordained as a deacon at the St. Charles Borromeo church in Visalia, Calif., last year.

item.title: China Confirms It Will Buy 200 Boeing Planes
item.description: Beijing’s acknowledgment on Wednesday that tariffs were discussed in talks with President Trump signals it was prepared to push back if the U.S. revives duties.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Catie Edmondson
item.media:description: President Trump attending a state banquet with China’s top leader, Xi Jinping, in Beijing last week.

item.title: Trump’s Stock Trades Raise Eyebrows. The Family Says He Keeps His Hands Off.
item.description: The Trump Organization says the president’s financial investments are handled by outside firms and that it has no control over the timing or selection of transactions.
item.dc:creator: Ben Protess
item.media:description: A flurry of trading activity has cast a spotlight on President Trump’s sprawling investment portfolio.

item.title: Tickle Me Elmo and the ‘Elmo-Mania’ That Consumed America
item.description: In 1996, the giggling stuffed doll turned holiday shopping into chaos, a preview of every sneaker drop, PlayStation launch and Taylor Swift ticket rush to come.
item.dc:creator: Amisha Padnani
item.media:credit: Steve Hart for The New York Times
item.media:description: There was a mad rush to grab one of the few remaining Tickle Me Elmo dolls at a Toys “R” Us store in Manhattan on Dec. 4, 1996.

item.title: Greg Hyman, Co-Creator of Tickle Me Elmo, Dies at 78
item.description: An electronics wizard, he was already a veteran inventor when he collaborated with Ron Dubren on an idea for a toy that giggled. The rest is retail history.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times; The Strong National Museum of Play
item.media:description: Greg Hyman in 2012. The toy that he and Ron Dubren designed for Tyco not only giggled and spoke — “That tickles!” — but vibrated when tickled three times.

item.title: Bond Yields Hit Highest Level Since 2007 as Inflation Fears Set In
item.description: The 30-year U.S. Treasury yield hasn’t been this high since the lead-up to the global financial crisis. Across Europe and Asia, yields are also elevated.
item.dc:creator: Joe Rennison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Powered by A.I., Google Changes Its Search Box for the First Time in 25 Years
item.description: Using a new Gemini A.I. model, the tech giant is overhauling its search box dimensions to answer longer queries, adding a video-generation tool and simplifying online shopping.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle, Kate Conger and Brian X. Chen
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: On Tuesday, Google said the A.I. shift had inspired it to overhaul its search bar for the first time since 2001.

item.title: Son of Mango Fashion Chain’s Founder Is Arrested as Suspect in His Death
item.description: Isak Andic, the billionaire who built the Spanish brand, died while hiking with his son in 2024. News outlets had reported that the son was under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Carlos Barragán
item.media:description: Jonathan Andic being escorted to court in Martorell, Spain, near Barcelona, on Tuesday.

item.title: Inflation Fears Cloud G7 Economic Agenda as Iran War Persists
item.description: The United States and Europe were at odds over the Trump administration’s decision to ease oil sanctions on Russia.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Rising Energy Costs and Data Centers at Heart of NextEra’s Dominion Bid
item.description: NextEra’s proposed acquisition of Dominion Energy comes as Americans are paying a lot more for electricity, and data centers are demanding a lot more power from utilities.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.media:credit: Tristan Spinski for The New York Times
item.media:description: NextEra Energy’s acquisition of Dominion Energy would consolidate utility operations along much of the East Coast, which could shape electricity service for tens of millions of customers.

item.title: Amazon E-Bikes Take Root in Manhattan and Brooklyn
item.description: Small companies that handle the final delivery are relying on them to skirt New York City traffic. Amazon plans to try the bikes elsewhere, too.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis and Vincent Alban

item.title: China Wants A.I. to Flourish, but Not at the Expense of Jobs
item.description: A series of precedent-setting rulings signals that Chinese courts are being enlisted to shield workers from displacement by artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
item.media:description: A robot learning to unpack and fold boxes at a lab working with Meituan in Shenzhen in January.

item.title: Tesla’s Semi Truck could Jolt the Trucking Industry
item.description: California truckers have expressed strong interest in the Tesla Semi because it costs much less and can travel further on a charge than electric trucks sold by established manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing and Gabriela Bhaskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Screens on either side of a Tesla Semi’s steering wheel provide a view of the traffic around the vehicle.

item.title: ‘The Future of Truth’ Contains Quotes Made Up by A.I.
item.description: Steven Rosenbaum, author of “The Future of Truth,” said he had started his own investigation after The New York Times asked about the fake quotes.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin
item.category: The Future of Truth: How AI Reshapes Reality (Book)
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
item.media:description: OpenAI’s ChatGPT on a laptop. The rise of A.I. has set off fears among publishers that they may accidentally release books from authors who improperly use A.I.-generated language.

item.title: Broadway’s ‘Giant,’ With John Lithgow as Roald Dahl, Turns a Profit in 10 Weeks
item.description: The fast success of this play, about the children’s author Roald Dahl, is a rarity on Broadway, where most shows lose money.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.media:description: John Lithgow as Roald Dahl in “Giant.”

item.title: In Musk vs. OpenAI, the Real Winner Was the Suit
item.description: It was the one thing on which every major player could agree.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for the New York Times, Josh Edelson/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Starbucks Korea CEO Fired Over ‘Tank Day’ Ad Evoking Country’s Brutal History
item.description: A marketing campaign called “Tank Day” coincided with the anniversary of a military dictatorship’s crackdown on pro-democracy demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.media:description: A Starbucks in Seoul, last week.

item.title: A FOMO Moment for Mythos
item.description: Those without access to Anthropic’s artificial intelligence model worry they’re more vulnerable to cyber attacks, a concern the company is seeking to address.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe, Ian Mount and Peter Coy
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: The debate over access to Mythos, Anthropic’s powerful artificial intelligence tool, has become a globe-spanning one.

item.title: As OpenAI Celebrates Court Win Against Musk, Other Challenges Lie Ahead
item.description: A jury’s rejection of Elon Musk’s $150 billion lawsuit against OpenAI was a major hurdle crossed. But the maker of ChatGPT faces a list of other problems.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Natallie Rocha
item.media:description: Lawyers for OpenAI, who won a resounding victory on Monday, outside the federal courthouse in Oakland, Calif.

item.title: Bipartisan Bill Would Impose New Annual Fee on Electric Vehicles
item.description: A House transportation bill introduced this week would require owners of electric cars to pay $130 to cover the cost of road repairs.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times
item.media:description: Congress is considering imposing a new annual fee on electric vehicles to help pay for road repairs.

item.title: Calls for ‘No Seed Oil’ Push Companies to Order Up Butter and Beef Tallow
item.description: Businesses are finding different (and more costly) ways to fry foods as shoppers demand alternatives to seed oils as part of the Make America Healthy Again movement.
item.dc:creator: Julie Creswell

item.title: On Blind, Anxious Tech Workers Get the Lowdown on Layoffs
item.description: On the website Blind, professionals share advice — and gallows humor — anonymously. It is chronicling the curdling of tech optimism.
item.dc:creator: Noam Scheiber
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sunguk Moon, a founder and the chief executive of Blind, said sentiment on the site “went from personal career planning to mass anxiety.”

item.title: Top Treasury Lawyer Resigns After Creation of ‘Anti-Weaponization Fund’
item.description: Brian Morrissey, the department’s general counsel, stepped down hours after the Trump administration announced the $1.8 billion fund.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren
item.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Treasury Department’s general counsel, Brian Morrissey, resigned on Monday, three people familiar with the move said.

item.title: Mamdani, the ‘Tax-the-Rich’ Mayor, Reaches Out to Titans of Finance
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who has angered some corporate executives with his tax policies, has had a recent series of meetings with top business leaders.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein and Lauren Hirsch

item.title: TrumpRx Adds Generic Drugs, With Mark Cuban, GoodRx and Amazon
item.description: President Trump announced the addition of 600 medicines to his online drugstore as he appeals to Americans concerned about high drug prices and affordability.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins
item.media:description: President Trump and Mark Cuban during an event at the White House on Monday.

item.title: New York Times Sues Pentagon for a Second Time
item.description: The Times is challenging a new requirement that reporters covering the military complex have an official escort, part of a broader legal challenge to the Pentagon’s press restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Erik Wemple
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The lawsuit is the second one from The New York Times over the Defense Department’s restrictions on the press.

item.title: Federal Prosecutors Move to Drop Bribery Case Against Indian Magnate
item.description: Gautam Adani had been accused of lying to American investors about payoffs in India. The end of the case came as President Trump has signaled a transactional approach to justice.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar and Alex Travelli
item.media:description: Gautam Adani’s companies have powered India’s rise as a global economic power.

item.title: Edmund Phelps, Who Upended the Way We View Inflation, Dies at 92
item.description: He won a Nobel in 2006 for challenging the conventional wisdom among economists that higher inflation was a necessary price to pay for low unemployment.
item.dc:creator: Chris Kornelis
item.media:description: Edmund S. Phelps in 2009. In a landmark 1968 paper, Dr. Phelps argued for a more complex view of the relationship between inflation and employment.

item.title: Shakira Is Found Not Guilty of Tax Fraud in Spain
item.description: A Spanish court ruled that the pop star was not a tax resident of Spain in 2011 and ordered the country’s tax authorities to return tens of millions of dollars to her.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses and Carlos Barragán
item.media:description: The pop star Shakira has been embroiled in a yearslong tax battle with the Spanish authorities.

item.title: Justice Dept. Sets Up $1.8 Billion Fund That Could Funnel Money to Trump Allies
item.description: The arrangement was denounced by critics as a slush fund for supporters of President Trump, possibly including Jan. 6 rioters.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush, Andrew Duehren and Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump, two of his sons and his family business had argued in the case that the Internal Revenue Service should have done more to prevent a former contractor from leaking tax information.

item.title: Bobby Darin Musical Reaches a Rare Broadway Milestone: Profit
item.description: “Just in Time,” which for a year starred Jonathan Groff, is the first new musical from last season to make money for investors.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.media:description: A performance at last year’s Tony Awards ceremony helped bring the show to national attention.

item.title: The Villain of This Year’s Commencement Speeches: A.I.
item.description: College students have interrupted graduation ceremonies to voice their fears about artificial intelligence. They’re not the only ones who are worried.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe and Ian Mount
item.media:description: Boos rained down on Eric Schmidt, Google’s former C.E.O., when he mentioned artificial intelligence in a commencement address yesterday.

item.title: G7 Finance Ministers Look to Contain Iran Economic Fallout
item.description: Top policymakers were expected to discuss rising energy prices and sanctions policy at a critical summit in Paris this week.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, greeting Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent last week in Beijing. Mr. Bessent is leading the U.S. delegation at the G7 finance ministers’ meeting in Paris this week.

item.title: My Boss Is an Alcoholic. What Should I Do?
item.description: Plus, a bona fide “kids say the darnedest things” moment at a company party.
item.dc:creator: Max Read
item.media:credit: Photo illustration by Margeaux Walter for The New York Times

item.title: Trump’s Approval Sinks Amid Unpopular War, Darkening G.O.P. Prospects
item.description: With the midterms nearing, President Trump’s approval rating has hit a second-term low as voters question his handling of the economy, according to the latest New York Times/Siena poll.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Insurers’ Delays in Approving Medical Care Persist, Despite Promises
item.description: Doctors and patients complain that the controversial practice of prior authorization for treatment and procedures is still widespread.
item.dc:creator: Reed Abelson
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield Assn
item.media:credit: Lindsay D'Addato for The New York Times
item.media:description: Candace Rond, center, with her daughters Gabby, left, and Sophia, who both have juvenile arthritis.

item.title: Oil Prices Climb and Bonds Falter as Iran War Raises Inflation Fears
item.description: Stocks and bonds wavered after President Trump issued a new warning to Iran.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Catastrophe Is Emerging in the World’s Most Vulnerable Places
item.description: The humanitarian relief system, decimated by cuts, faces a grave challenge as the Middle East war causes soaring costs for food, fuel and fertilizer.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Goodman and Finbarr O’Reilly
item.category: United States Agency for International Development
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Muslima Ibrahim Mohamed, 38, holding her 2-day-old son, Noor Mohamed.
